text.title: brainstorming-ideation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for brainstorming-ideation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the same agent that generated the ideas to also dot-vote or rank them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The generating agent has higher confidence in ideas it produced with higher certainty in the first pass. Self-scoring compounds this into a self-reinforcing ranking with no independent judgment.

failure-mode.description: Including evaluation language ("good idea because...", "this works better than option B") in the generation prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The model self-censors and produces fewer, safer ideas. The generation pass becomes a mixed pass that produces a filtered list masquerading as a brainstorm.

failure-mode.description: Accepting cluster assignments without human review, particularly when two conceptually distinct approaches share domain vocabulary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cosine similarity clusters by word overlap. "Automate onboarding email sequence" and "Automate onboarding task assignment" may cluster together despite being architecturally different solutions.

failure-mode.description: Passing a list of 50+ ideas to a scoring prompt in a single call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ideas in positions 1-15 receive systematically higher scores than identical ideas in positions 35-50 due to positional bias in LLM attention. The first batch is not comparable to the last batch.
